title: SDLC Artifact Manifest
description: Formalizes skills/artifact-manifest/SKILL.md's Output Format — the per-product manifest instance at artifacts/[product]/_manifest.json. This validates the PRODUCT MANIFEST (what was actually produced), not the master artifact-type catalog (the plugin-wide, product-agnostic list of what CAN be produced, which the skill maintains as a prose table and does not have a separate JSON representation — see artifact-manifest's 'Two Registries' section). Written and maintained by the post-artifact-created hook.

id.description: Stable per the ID scheme in artifact-manifest: <product-slug>-<artifact-type>-<sequence>, e.g. dataestate-adr-001. Assigned once at creation, never changed — even across a rename, a path move, or a supersession. Pattern is intentionally the general slug shape rather than a rigid 3-segment regex, since a revision suffix (e.g. '-r2', per the skill's own worked example) is a legitimate extension of the base ID.

type.description: Matches a row in the master artifact-type catalog (skills/artifact-manifest/SKILL.md). Not a closed enum here — the catalog is described as extended by new skills over time, so this schema does not hardcode the ~21-row representative cross-section as an exhaustive list.

path.description: Current file (or directory, for source-tree artifacts) path, relative to repo root. Must resolve to a real file/dir per the Quality Criteria's 'Manifest matches filesystem' check — this schema validates shape only, not filesystem existence.

version.description: Semantic version, matching the artifact's own version frontmatter field.

superseded_by.description: The ID of the artifact that replaced this one. Present only when status is superseded, but not schema-enforced as conditionally required — an agent may mark an entry superseded without a replacement yet existing (e.g. a deprecated artifact being retired outright).

producing_agent.description: The agent that produced this artifact, or 'any agent' for cross-cutting types like risk-register entries any agent may write.

traces_to.description: CLAUDE.md's Artifact Standards traceability requirement, made concrete. An artifact with no traces_to is an orphan (see artifact-manifest's Anti-Patterns).

ref.description: Must resolve to an actual requirement/story ID, a decision ID in sdlc-context.json's decisions array, or a named Domain Event — never a vague placeholder like 'requirements' (see Anti-Patterns: Traceability theater).
